summary: FPV drones struck a Russian Ka-52 helicopter near Nadiivka, Donetsk region, eliminating the crew after a crash landing and hitting them again during an evacuation attempt. A separate FPV strike targeted a VSU BUK air defense system on Bolshoy Sokolny Island. Ukrainian forces are experimenting with tactics like startling Russian troops before strikes and using multiple drones to hit a single target, as seen when two FPVs struck a soldier behind a tree. A Russian infantryman was filmed charging at a Ukrainian FPV drone with a bayonet and was killed. The 59th Brigade is reported to have downed another Ka-52 on the Pokrovsk front, with Zelensky awarding the Order of Courage, 2nd class, to the troops involved. Outside Ukraine, an Iranian-backed militia used a fiber-optic FPV drone to attack the US Victory Base near Baghdad.
